summary: Read-only R3 review from RAG/search-quality lens. Inspected DinoBrain memory registry entries, OS remaining gaps plan, completion conditions, RAG OS consensus, package scripts, and retrieval/RAG eval/proof/goal/health source files. Verdict: AGREE on the plan's completion conditions, with implementation-risk notes around health/status alignment and scaffold RAG not being sufficient outside verify:goal.
decisions: The docs require real semantic dense retrieval or documented local semantic model, hybrid sparse+dense, rerank/contribution metrics, memory-on/off generated-answer evaluation, and Ragas-like answer-quality metrics before completion.

The code currently marks rag:proof/eval scaffold artifacts healthy in their own reports, but verify:goal correctly blocks completion when semantic_embedding_provider is false, provider is local_text_hashing_v1, caveats indicate deterministic canary, or generated-answer quality evidence is missing.
Risk to carry forward: status:health treats rag_proof/rag_eval status=healthy as healthy, so P1-11 health/Observatory alignment must be implemented before relying on health alone.
next_steps: When implementing P1-10/P1-11, make status:health and Observatory expose the same completion-grade RAG blocker model as verify:goal, including semantic provider, text-hash scaffold, deterministic canary caveats, and generated-answer metrics.
search_queries: DinoBrain rag retrieval hybrid dense rerank contextual eval canary Ragas semantic memory-on memory-off
semantic_embedding_provider local_text_hashing_v1 hybrid_contextual_v2 dense BM25 RRF rerank generated answer quality verify:goal
